name: INCLUSION CYST OF SPINE
definition: uni- / multilocular benign cystic tumor lined by stratified squamous epithelium containing cystic tumor lined by a membrane composed of epidermal elements of skin

demographics.Path: (a)   wall ± calcifications (in 31%) and papillary projections along inner surface
demographics.Prevalence: 1-2% of intraspinal tumors; 0.7-1.8% of all CNS tumors

dominant_finding: almost always complete spinal block on myelography
discriminating_features: almost always complete spinal block on myelography
uni- / multilocular cystic lesion with variable SI:

CT: almost always complete spinal block on myelography
MR: uni- / multilocular cystic lesion with variable SI:
hyperintense on T1WI + hypointense on Gd-enhanced fat-suppressed T1WI (fatty
occasionally hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI (fluid secretions from
NO contrast enhancement (except for small soft-tissue component)
dissemination of lipid droplets scattered throughout CSF and intra-axially throughout
displacement of spinal cord / nerve roots
NO internal enhancement + faint to NO rim enhancement
asymmetric filling defect / complete spinal block
variable signal intensity ← differing concentrations of cyst content of keratin + water +
T1 isointense (especially when tumor small) to slightly hyperintense relative to CSF
iso- to slightly hyperintense relative to CSF on T2WI
T1 hyperintense + T2 hypointense "white epidermoid" (rare) ← high protein
hyperintense to CSF on FLAIR and D WI ← restricted diffusion (DD x: arachnoid cyst (isointense

clinical.Cx: spontaneous / intraoperative / traumatic dermoid cyst rupture → lipid droplets
clinical.Prognosis: high risk of morbidity + mortality (with rupture)
